scope.objective: Privilege escalation to root via world-writable /usr/bin/bash replacement. Deploy SUID-dropping wrapper, find or wait for root trigger, obtain root shell, read /root/root.txt.
in_scope: /tmp/ for staging payloads and SUID bash copy
Monitoring for SUID creation (inotifywait or polling)
Arcane API on localhost:3552 (to trigger root bash execution if needed)
stop_conditions: Wrapper deployed but no trigger after exhausting all trigger candidates

primary_path: Replace /usr/bin/bash with a wrapper script that: (1) copies /tmp/bash_orig to /tmp/rootbash, (2) sets SUID bit (chmod u+s /tmp/rootbash), (3) executes the real bash (/tmp/bash_orig) with original args so the caller doesn't notice. Then wait/trigger root to execute bash. Once /tmp/rootbash exists with SUID, run /tmp/rootbash -p to get root shell. Read /root/root.txt.
backup_path: If no automatic trigger fires: try to trigger root bash execution by interacting with Arcane API (e.g., POST to container restart/update endpoints — Arcane runs as root and may shell out to bash). Also check if 'su -' or SSH login as root triggers bash (root's shell is /bin/bash per /etc/passwd).

vulnerability_primitive.primitive: World-writable /usr/bin/bash binary (mode 0777) — any user can replace the bash binary with arbitrary code
delivery_forms: Replace /usr/bin/bash with shell script wrapper that stages SUID copy before calling real bash
Replace /usr/bin/bash with compiled ELF binary that does the same
Replace /usr/bin/bash with symlink to attacker-controlled script
vulnerability_primitive.defenses_observed: None — file is mode 0777, no immutable bit, no AppArmor profile on bash binary

process_model.background: Optional: inotifywait or polling loop watching for /tmp/rootbash SUID creation
process_model.critical_note: MUST backup real bash to /tmp/bash_orig BEFORE replacing (NOIRE reports /tmp/bash_orig already exists from prior session — verify md5 match first). If wrapper is malformed, bash breaks for ALL users including our SSH session. Test wrapper logic carefully. Keep a second SSH session open as fallback.

notes: NOIRE found that /tmp/bash_wrapper and /tmp/bash_orig already exist from a prior player. Verify they are intact before using — do NOT trust blindly. The real bash md5 should match /tmp/bash_orig. All cron scripts use /bin/sh (dash), not bash — so the trigger is NOT cron. Root's login shell IS /bin/bash per /etc/passwd, so any root login or 'su -' would trigger it. Arcane (Go binary, PID 1491, runs as root) may call bash for Docker operations — try triggering container updates or restarts via the API if unauthenticated endpoints exist. tmux session 'shell' has live SSH — attach with 'tmux attach -t shell'.
